title: Resume
text: Currently in my first year at Ashoka University, I am planning to major in Biology with a minor in Mathematics. Fascinated by the cellular and molecular mechanisms that control the human body and mind, I hope to pursue a future in medical research. I also plan to explore Computer Science and History during my time as an undergraduate. Simultaneously, I plan to foster a change in the STEM high school culture in India, shifting the focus from isolated teaching, memorization and entrance exam preparation, to the use of the scientific method in class, critical thinking and interdisciplinary learning.

title: Resume
text:
Education

Ashoka University

Duration: August 2024 - May 2028
Major: Biology
Minor: Mathematics



DriveChange Learning & Resource Centre (DLRC)

Duration: May 2022 - March 2024
Board: Cambridge International AS and A Levels.
A Levels Final Grades: Mathematics: A*, Physics: A*, Chemistry: A*, Biology: A*.



Vidya Valley School

Duration: June 2012 - May 2022
Board: Council for the Indian School Certificate Examinations

SAT Score

Date Taken: August 2023

title: Resume
text:
Clubs and Activities

The Biology Society at Ashoka University

Role and Duration: Active member from August 2024 - Present.
Key Initiatives: Contributed to the BioSoc Stall at “Run For A Cause,” an event organized by IEEE to raise funds for science kits for high school students in Asawarpur, and an engaging “Intro to Python” workshop.
Current Responsibilities: Currently one of the two organizers for upcoming flagship events: The ASP AMA (Ashoka Scholars’ Program Ask Me Anything) and the Bio Mixer.



Student Government

Role and Duration: Head Girl at Vidya Valley School from June 2021 - May 2022.
Leadership duties: As the Head of the Office Bearers, I led the Vidya Valley School Student Council.
Key Learnings: Learnt what it truly meant to be a leader - someone who didn’t simply take on all the work themselves, but encouraged and brought out the best in others. I hope that I’ve influenced other girls to take up leadership roles.

title: Resume
text:
Internships

NIUS Project | IISER Pune

Duration: June 2025 - August 2025
Selection: Out of around 40 participants at NIUS’s Biology Camp, approximately 10 of us were selected to pursue research projects under the mentorship of esteemed scientists.
Assignment: Assigned to Dr. Kundan Sengupta, Principle Investigator at the Chromosome Biology Lab (CBL) at IISER Pune, starting this summer.



Data Entry | ElixirAI

Duration: April 2024 - June 2024
Project: Contributed to a project aimed at digitizing paper-based surgery reports from Jehangir Hospital.
Responsibilities: Included reviewing physical records and inputting their data into a spreadsheet, and collaborating closely with the team to verify data accuracy and resolve discrepancies.
Key Learnings: Allowed me to develop a deeper understanding of data digitization, and gave me valuable insights into the intersection of healthcare and technology.



Teaching Assistant | DLRC

Duration: August 2022 - March 2024
Responsibilities: Tutored juniors struggling in math and chemistry, and took chemistry lessons for ninth and eleventh-graders. Conducted several practical sessions for eleventh-graders, and designed and corrected tests (IGCSE, AS & A Level Syllabus).
Key Learnings: Worked with one student for two years teaching math and chemistry, and she taught me what it meant to be a good teacher. This experience helped me uncover and nurture my love for teaching, which I will continue alongside my studies and career.



Lab Assistant | Symbiosis Centre For Waste Resource Management

Duration: April 2023
Project: Spent three weeks in a microbiology lab learning how to isolate and culture bacterial colonies from a leachate sample extracted from a landfill. The colonies would later go on to be screened for useful enzymes.
Key Learnings: Exposed to the different aspects of research and experimentation, getting a glimpse of lab work. Using my theoretical knowledge in my practical work, I understood the importance of developing intuition, technique and patience.

title: Resume
text:
Portfolio

Online Course: The Addicted Brain

Platform: Coursera
Duration: December 2022 - February 2023
Details: Completed an online course offered by Emory University on Coursera titled The Addicted Brain. Learnt about various drugs and their functional mechanisms, as well as substance abuse as a social issue.
Impact: Thanks to this course I developed an interest in biochemistry, neurosciences and pharmacology, inspiring my goal to pursue a career in medical research.



NIUS Biology Camp

Location & Date: Homi Bhabha Centre for Science Education, TIFR in November 2024.
Selection & Activities: Selected to attend the Biology Camp organized by the National Initiative of Undergraduate Science in Mumbai, where we had the opportunity to conduct various experiments in the lab. We were later divided into groups, assigned specific inquiries, and tasked with exploring them through experimentation and literature reviews.
Impact: Allowed me to meet aspiring biologists from across the country and independently conduct multiple experiments, significantly enhancing my skills and knowledge.

title: Resume
text:
Awards

Wg Cdr Suhas Clement Solomon Award for Extraordinary Achievement: Awarded in December 2019 at Vidya Valley School. Every year, this award is given by Vidya Valley School to one student for an all round extraordinary performance. The trophy bears the inscription: “To always aim for the stars with grit, determination and, above all, with a sense of humour.” I am the first girl in the school’s history to have received this prestigious award, and becoming a role model for young girls means a lot to me.
Head Girl: June 2021 - May 2022, Vidya Valley School.
National Silver Medal, Taekwondo: August 2019, Gujarat, India.
Black Belt, Taekwondo: April 2019, Fighters Taekwondo Academy.

title: About
text:
Currently in my first year at Ashoka University, I am planning to major in Biology with a minor in Mathematics. Fascinated by the cellular and molecular mechanisms that control the human body and mind, I hope to pursue a future in medical research. I also plan to explore Computer Science and History during my time as an undergraduate.
Simultaneously, I plan to foster a change in the STEM high school culture in India, shifting the focus from isolated teaching, memorization and entrance exam preparation, to the use of the scientific method in class, critical thinking and interdisciplinary learning.
